page.content: As of December 2023, COSCO Shipping's Newbuild order book comprises 45 total orders, with a total order value of $3.5B, including 15 bulk carriers scheduled for delivery in 2025, 20 container ships set for 2026, and 10 tankers expected in 2027.

lines.text: As of December 2023, COSCO Shipping's Newbuild order book comprises 45 total orders, with a total order value of $3.5B,
including 15 bulk carriers scheduled for delivery in 2025, 20 container ships set for 2026, and 10 tankers expected in 2027.

page.content: Contracts signed account for 30 units, representing 67%, while pending orders total 15 units, at 33%. Completed deliveries stand at 5 units, or 11%.

page.content: The vessel age profile shows a total fleet size of 100 vessels with a total value of $500M and an average age of 12 years. In the age group of 0-5 years, there are 30 vessels with a market share of 30% and a year-over-year change of 5% and a scrapping rate of 10%. The 6-10 years age group contains 25 vessels, also with a market share of 25%, a year-over-year change of 6%, and a scrapping rate of 12%. The 11-15 years age group has 20 vessels and a market share of 20%. The 16-20 years category includes 15 vessels with a market share of 15%, while the 21+ years category represents 10 vessels with a market share of 10%. Total scrapped vessels amount to 15, with a scrapping value of $75M and a scrapping year-over-year change of 5%. The projected age profile for December 2024 indicates a scrapping forecast of 17%. The retention rate is 80%, with total new orders at 20 units and a new orders value of $250M, contributing to market share and projected growth percentages.
